title: Amaryllis canterai
taxonomy: Amaryllis canterai is not a well-documented species, however, Amaryllis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Amaryllidaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family is further classified under the order Asparagales in the class Liliopsida. The order Asparagales is part of the clade monocots, which is a sub-classification of the larger clade called angiosperms. The angiosperms belong to the larger group of plants known as the embryophytes, which in turn belong to the kingdom Plantae. The Amaryllidaceae family is native to the Southern Hemisphere, particularly in Africa, South America, and Australia.

constituents: Amaryllis canterai, which is also known as the Naked Lady or Belladonna Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family.
